title: S4: migrate okf-profile.md into the bundle it describes
action: Per G6, the last piece of the old model still pretending to be current truth: docs/specs/okf-profile.md — the spec that DEFINES the bundle — lives outside it. Migrate it with the standard loop, twelfth time, exactly as commit 8710d03 (doctrine-layer) and the ten that followed. STEP 0: capture the pin BEFORE any edit — `git rev-parse HEAD` as pin commit, copy the source verbatim to docs/history/okf-migration-f2/sources/okf-profile.md, `git hash-object` it as blob_sha, run --inventory and record counts AND unparsed_blocks, add the pin to PIN_REGISTRY with the right scheme and full expected_counts INCLUDING unparsed_blocks. It carries B1-B12 behaviors plus Business Rules and Edge Cases, so ba-nine-section is the expected scheme — but MEASURE, do not assume: if the inventory shows heavy unparsed blocks it is not conforming and you must report rather than force (F9). STEP 1: author concepts under docs/knowledge/areas/okf-profile/, split by TOPIC after reading the whole source; every anchor lands in exactly one concept's bee.sources; canonical frontmatter through emitFrontmatter; unique bee.id; one bee.authoritative_for per subject — and note the anti-fork gate is now live and will refuse a duplicate claim, so pick subjects carefully. KEEP TERMINOLOGY VERBATIM (the fidelity floor is lexical). STEP 2: replace the source with a pointer stub carrying the FULL anchor map and the migrated_to frontmatter the fence keys on (docs/specs/okf_specs_fence recognises stubs structurally — a stub without migrated_to FAILS the fence). Update reading-map.md. Grep skills/ scripts/ hooks/ .bee/bin/ AGENTS.md docs/specs/ for citations into okf-profile and rewire live ones; docs/history/** stays archive. NOTE: the fence's own allowlist classifies okf-profile.md as `profile` — once it becomes a stub the fence must still pass, so check whether that classification needs to become `stub` and adjust the fence if so, with its selftest updated. STEP 3: add the --check okf-profile entry to EXTRA_SUITES and MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS. STEP 4: bee knowledge index, log.md dated entry, full chain. RED-FIRST: run the gate with one anchor deliberately unassigned and capture the LOST output.

truths: red evidence: the gate RED with one anchor unassigned — output quoted
coverage: every anchor owned exactly once, 0 duplicated, 0 lost — counts quoted
fidelity min/median quoted above the 0.60 floor with no concept edited
the fence still PASSES after okf-profile.md becomes a stub — its classification handled explicitly and its selftest updated if changed
knowledge check 0 errors 0 warnings; index --check 0 stale
pin declares unparsed_blocks; blob_sha verified against the committed copy
prohibitions: never force a scheme if the inventory shows the source is not conforming — report instead (F9)
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the fidelity floor
never leave the fence in a state where a stub without migrated_to would pass

trace.outcome: G6 closed: docs/specs/okf-profile.md — the spec that DEFINES the bundle — migrated INTO it as five topic-split bee.area concepts under docs/knowledge/areas/okf-profile/, leaving a D37 pointer stub with the full 24-row anchor map. 24 anchors, 24 owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost against pinned blob 9267d3e@53d8111; fidelity min/median/max 1.000 vs the 0.60 floor with no concept edited. rules:0 is measured not forced — the nine un-ided Business Rules bullets are counted unparsed (D10 forbids inventing ids) and migrated verbatim anyway; unparsed_blocks:17 pinned, including the 3 from the extractor's code-fence blind spot in the Templates section, so a future fence-aware extractor fails loudly instead of silently reshaping. The fence's okf-profile.md named exception was REMOVED, not relabelled — it now passes as an ordinary structural stub, asserted both ways in selftest. Citations rewired in bee-scribing, bee-hive, inject.mjs, test_bundle_mode.mjs and reading-map.md. Chain 79 suites green, SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT 60→62.

  "chain": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 79 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=59323ms, exit 0",
  "red_first": "Deliberately removed P7 from overview.md's bee.sources and ran the gate: 'FAIL okf_migrate --check okf-profile: 24 anchors, 23 owned, 0 duplicated, 1 lost' / '- LOST in concepts: P7 is claimed by no concept's bee.sources (expected one owner)'. Restored, gate GREEN again.",
  "coverage": "PASS okf_migrate --check okf-profile: 24 anchors, 24 owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost. Ground truth DERIVED from pinned blob 9267d3eaef2cc016d32a96bca1e6d99822bca3c3 (53d81117:docs/specs/okf-profile.md, via git, scheme ba-nine-section) with counts asserted {behaviors:13, rules:0, edges:4, pointers:7, total:24, unparsed_blocks:17}.",
  "fidelity": "fidelity floor 0.60 (F11): 24 anchors measured against their owning concept - min 1.000, median 1.000, max 1.000 normalized token overlap with the pinned blob. No concept edited, no threshold touched.",
  "telemetry": "F12: anchors_per_concept 4.80, concepts_per_100_source_lines 0.82 (24 anchors, 5 concepts, 612 source lines) - inside the [0.5x, 2x] outlier band of the running median across 10 pinned ba-nine-section sources. Bundle invariants (authority uniqueness, zero not_canonical, index freshness) all hold.",
  "fence": "okf-profile.md REMOVED from NAMED_EXCEPTIONS rather than relabelled, and now passes as an ordinary STRUCTURAL stub via the migrated_to branch. Selftest asserts both directions: the real stub passes as 'stub', and an okf-profile.md WITHOUT migrated_to fails as new content. okf_specs_fence --selftest and --check both green in the chain.",
  "bundle": "node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge check --json -> {errors:0, profile_errors:0, warnings:0} over 139 files / 121 concepts. knowledge index --check: 17 expected index file(s), 0 stale.",
  "pin": "PIN_REGISTRY['okf-profile'] declares unparsed_blocks: 17 with every one of the 17 identified by line before any edit (5 in Behaviors & Operations, 9 un-ided Business Rules bullets, 3 from the code-fence hazard in the Templates section). blob_sha 9267d3ea... verified against the committed copy at docs/history/okf-migration-f2/sources/okf-profile.md.",
  "f9": "rules: 0 is MEASURED, not forced. The nine ## Business Rules bullets carry no R<n> id, so D10 forbids inventing them; they are counted unparsed and migrated verbatim into the concept whose subject each states, never anchor-gated. Nothing was reshaped to make ba-nine-section fit.",
  "closeout": "Mirror + manifest close-out completed this session: .bee/bin/lib/inject.mjs re-synced from templates/lib (test_lib_mirror green, 27 files byte-identical), plugin skill trees re-rendered (128 files x2), release manifest re-written (437 files match), self-onboard re-run to refresh the managed-hash ledger (ledger_parity clean).",
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (git show 53d8111:docs/specs/okf-profile.md): the profile was 611 lines of live area truth sitting OUTSIDE the bundle it defines, and scripts/okf_specs_fence.mjs granted it a name-based pass ('profile' classification in NAMED_EXCEPTIONS) that would have kept saying yes to any content under that filename. Pre-change checks that FAILED against the new contract: (1) the coverage gate did not exist for this area at all — 'node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --check okf-profile' at 53d8111 had no PIN_REGISTRY entry and could not grade a single anchor; (2) the fence selftest had no assertion that an okf-profile.md WITHOUT migrated_to fails, so the silent-rot branch was untested; (3) the RED-first proof this session: with P7 unassigned the gate reports 'FAIL okf_migrate --check okf-profile: 24 anchors, 23 owned, 0 duplicated, 1 lost / LOST in concepts: P7 is claimed by no concept's bee.sources', demonstrating the gate bites before it was allowed to pass."
}
